text.title: GitHub Copilot Workspace Four-Gate Pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the tracker-github-copilot-workspace methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every Workspace-driven PR MUST pass four gates in order: spec → plan → diff → PR. Skipping or merging gates is a hard refusal.
rule.rationale: The four-gate sequence is the only mechanism that gives reviewers durable artifacts at each abstraction layer; skipping gates collapses the pipeline.

rule.statement: The human MUST have an explicit edit point at each gate. Auto-progression that bypasses the edit step is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without an edit gate, the human becomes a rubber-stamp; the gate ceases to be a control.

rule.statement: Every Workspace PR MUST include an auto-attached comment with a read-only snapshot link to the Workspace that produced the diff.
rule.rationale: Reviewers cannot interrogate the spec/plan from the diff alone; the snapshot link is the bridge.

rule.statement: Agents configured to go straight from issue to diff (bypassing spec + plan) MUST abort with a non-zero exit. CI rejects PRs without Workspace metadata.
rule.rationale: Without an abort, the gates become optional; teams will skip them under pressure.

rule.statement: Workspace is required for PRs touching > 1 file OR adding > 50 LoC. Trivial fixes MAY bypass with a `trivial:` PR prefix.
rule.rationale: Forcing Workspace on every typo fix drains velocity; the gate exists for non-trivial work.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
